metadata_checks.comments: Metadata match the article. It is a systematic review centered on carbon taxes/CO2 taxes and related fuel-tax evidence, even though it also reviews cap-and-trade.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Higher energy prices and less purchasing power
determinant_checks.comment: Directly reported in Table 1 and linked to lower acceptability in Section 3.2.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Loss of jobs
determinant_checks.comment: Direct tax-cost concern; Table 2 reports negative acceptability effects.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Policy does not limit freedom of choice
determinant_checks.comment: Directly tied to higher acceptability in Table 2/Section 3.2.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Neutral cost distribution (all citizens pay the same share of income)
determinant_checks.comment: Direct distributional fairness determinant with positive acceptability effects.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Regressive incidence (all citizens pay the same amount; effects on the poor)
determinant_checks.comment: Directly tax-specific and negatively associated with acceptability.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Progressive incidence (higher-income citizens pay a larger share of income)
determinant_checks.comment: Directly tax-specific and positively associated with acceptability.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Exemptions and privileges given to some sectors
determinant_checks.comment: Direct policy-design fairness concern affecting acceptability.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Satisfaction with governmental information provision about the policy
determinant_checks.comment: Procedural determinant directly linked to higher acceptability.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Trust in government
determinant_checks.comment: Procedural determinant directly linked to higher acceptability.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Limited understanding of the policy
determinant_checks.comment: Directly reported procedural/knowledge barrier to acceptability.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Overall fairness
determinant_checks.comment: Direct overarching fairness judgment positively affects acceptability.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Environmental projects
determinant_checks.comment: Revenue-use determinant; directly tied to carbon-tax acceptability.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Funding technological innovation and energy efficiency
determinant_checks.comment: Revenue recycling choice directly linked to acceptability.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Redistributing revenue to vulnerable groups (low income households, elderly)
determinant_checks.comment: Direct revenue-recycling determinant with positive acceptability effects.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Equal share of revenue given to each taxpayer
determinant_checks.comment: Direct revenue-recycling determinant with acceptability effects.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Environmental tax reform (reducing existing taxes)
determinant_checks.comment: Direct revenue-recycling design choice affecting acceptability.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Tax rebate
determinant_checks.comment: Direct revenue-recycling option with mixed acceptability effects.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Funding public transportation
determinant_checks.comment: Direct revenue-use determinant tied to tax acceptability.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Mitigate negative impacts of climate change
determinant_checks.comment: Revenue-use option reported in Table 4; still tax-linked.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: General budget
determinant_checks.comment: Direct revenue-use option negatively associated with acceptability.

missing_tax_determinants.evidence_quote: Less comfort or wellbeing 1 (de Groot and Schuitema, 2012)
missing_tax_determinants.comment: Direct personal-cost concern omitted from extraction.

missing_tax_determinants.raw_name_in_paper: Voluntary behaviour punished rather than appreciated
missing_tax_determinants.evidence_quote: Voluntary behaviour punished rather than appreciated
missing_tax_determinants.comment: Motivational crowding-out concern is tax-specific and absent.

missing_tax_determinants.raw_name_in_paper: Assuring that large industries are subject to a carbon price
missing_tax_determinants.evidence_quote: People who accepted the carbon tax ... had a higher belief in assuring that large industries are subject to a carbon price

missing_tax_determinants.raw_name_in_paper: Expected cooperation in energy conservation’s effort from fellow-citizens
missing_tax_determinants.evidence_quote: Expected cooperation in energy conservation’s effort from fellow-citizens 1 (Baranzini and Carattini, 2017)

missing_tax_determinants.raw_name_in_paper: Policy burden shared among provinces, states or countries
missing_tax_determinants.evidence_quote: Policy burden shared among provinces, states or countries 1 (Brannlund and Persson, 2012)
Responsibility for future generations 1 (Beuermann and Santarius, 2006*)
missing_tax_determinants.comment: Direct fairness motive omitted from extraction.

missing_tax_determinants.evidence_quote: Impacts on rural households n/a 1 (Baranzini and Carattini, 2017)
missing_tax_determinants.comment: Rural-burden concern is tax-specific and missing.

missing_tax_determinants.evidence_quote: Fund improvements to infrastructure (roads, bridges...) 1 (Kaplowitz and McCright, 2015)
missing_tax_determinants.comment: Direct revenue-use determinant absent from extraction.

final_recommendation_summary: Keep the paper in the corpus. Metadata are correct and the extracted items are tax-linked, but the extraction is incomplete: several direct carbon-tax acceptability determinants from Tables 2 and 4 are missing, especially comfort/wellbeing, voluntary-behaviour concerns, burden-sharing, rural households, future generations, and infrastructure spending.
